summary:
The pterygomandibular space primarily houses structures including nerves related to mandible function but excludes the long buccal nerve which lies elsewhere. \boxed{B}

The summary concisely captures the essential anatomical distinction regarding what belongs or doesn't belong in the specified anatomic region based directly upon provided knowledge about its contents versus peripheral locations for certain named neural pathways. Agent1 focused tightly on identifying unique features marking boundaries between different regions' typical innervation patterns.
